LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER BARBARA JOHNSTON, EXECUTIVE DIRECTOR, MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING COMPLIANCE WITH INVESTIGATIVE SUBPOENA (PER EX PARTE ORDER OF 01/05/10). THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS DENIED. THE SUBPOENAS ARE OVERBROAD, SEEKING ALL RECORDS DATING BACK TO JANUARY 1, 2008 ALTHOUGH THE SUSPECT PRESCRIPTIONS BEGAN IN JANUARY 2009. INSUFFICIENT FOUNDATION FOR DR. MILLER'S OPINIONS. RESPONDENT'S DOCTORS EXPLAIN QUESTIONED DOSAGES ARE SOMETIMES PROPER. (WOOD VS. SUPERIOR COURT OF SACRAMENTO COUNTY (1985) 166 CAL.APP. 3D 1138.) ORDER & JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Petitioner Barbara Johnston, Executive Director, Medical Board Of California'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Compliance With Investigative Subpoena (Per Ex Parte Order Of 01/05/10)
Off Calendar Feb-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Petitioner Barbara Johnston, Executive Director, Medical Board of California's Order to Show Cause Regarding Compliance with Investigative Subpoena (Per Ex Parte Order Of 01/05/10). Appearances: Kenneth L. Freeman, Attorney at Law, (415) 885-5728 appearing for Respondent. (The tentative ruling is not being contested.) The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied. The subpoenas are overbroad, seeking all records dating back to January 1, 2008 although the suspect prescriptions began in January 2009. Insufficient foundation for Dr. Miller's opinions. Respondent's doctors explain questioned dosages are sometimes proper. (Wood vs. Superior Court of Sacramento County (1985) 166 Cal.App. 3d 1138.) Order & Judgment signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Petitioner Barbara Johnston, Executive Director, Medical Board Of California'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Compliance With Investigative Subpoena (Per Ex Parte Order Of 01/05/10)
Off Calendar Feb-10-2010 Continued To Feb-24-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Petitioner Barbara Johnston, Executive Director, Medical Board of California's Order to Show Cause Regarding Compliance With Investigative Subpoena (Per Ex Parte Order Of 01/05/10). No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Matter continued to February 24, 2010 on the Court's own motion. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR # 5797
